summary: Large Corneria stage decomp/matching PR focused on src/melee/gr/grcorneria.c, adding many function bodies, local Corneria data structs/inlines, typed access for parts of Corneria ground vars, and related header/prototype adjustments. Bot report showed 7 new grcorneria matches and 18 unmatched-item improvements. Reviewer feedback called out raw __assert cleanup, pointer-math cleanup, use of ABS/MIN/MAX/CLAMP-style macros, and suspicious s32 writes into mostly f32 fields.

key_files.role: Primary decomp work: large addition of grCorneria function bodies, Corneria stage data structs, arwing/cannon/dialog data access, helper inlines, includes, PAD_STACK uses, JObj/GObj logic, and remaining raw pointer/cast areas discussed in review.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grCn_StageData and grCn_Data definitions; grCorneria_801DCCFC, grCorneria_801DD350, grCorneria_801DDAC4, grCorneria_801DDE88, grCorneria_801DE024, grCorneria_801DE568, grCorneria_801DE8E4, grCorneria_801E01A8, grCorneria_801E0678, grCorneria_801E08CC bodies
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists 7 new matches and 18 unmatched improvements in main/melee/gr/grcorneria
review_comments: all 4 review comments are on src/melee/gr/grcorneria.c

key_files.role: Ground/Corneria type adjustments supporting grcorneria field access; exact content not loaded in excerpt.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grcorneria.c accesses gp->gv.corneria.xE4 as Vec3 components and gp->gv.arwing/corneria union fields

what_changed.text: Replaced numerous grcorneria placeholder comments with C bodies, including initialization, arwing spawning/behavior, stage section movement, camera/position checks, animation/audio interactions, and dialog/joint handling.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: placeholder removals for grCorneria_801DCCFC, grCorneria_801DD350, grCorneria_801DDAC4, grCorneria_801DDE88, grCorneria_801DE024, grCorneria_801DE568, grCorneria_801DE8E4, grCorneria_801E01A8, grCorneria_801E0678, grCorneria_801E08CC

what_changed.text: Introduced local Corneria data modeling: grCn_StageData for stage parameters and grCn_Data for arwing_gobj, arwing_type, arwing_group, cannon/effect data, animation ids, positions, lookup tables, and dialog joints.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grCn_StageData fields x0..x88 and static grCn_StageData* grCn_804D69A0
diff_excerpt: grCn_Data fields arwing_gobj[3], arwing_type[3], arwing_group[3], cannon_x/cannon_y, effect_pos, anim_ids[14], positions[14], x3D8, x444, x458, dialog_joints[5], entries

what_changed.text: Added static inline helpers for repeated Corneria behaviors: random range selection, far-position checking, arwing spawning, and unique type picking.
Converted some previously scalar Corneria ground-var usage into Vec3-style field access, e.g. xE4.x/y/z replacing xE4/xE8/xEC initialization and reads.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grCorneria_801DD674 changes xE4/xE8/xEC to xE4.x/xE4.y/xE4.z
diff_excerpt: grCorneria_801DDCF0 uses gp->gv.corneria.xE4.x and xE4.y

what_changed.text: Added required includes for newly referenced subsystems and helpers, including ftlib, gm_1832, grdatfiles, item/itgreatfoxlaser, lbaudio_ax, gobjgxlink, and particle.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: include additions near top of src/melee/gr/grcorneria.c

what_changed.text: Automation report claimed measurable progress: 7 new grcorneria matches totaling +3544 matched bytes and 18 improvements in unmatched grcorneria items, with overall matched code +0.09%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment by decomp-dev[bot]: Report for GALE01, matched code 61.81% (+0.09%, +3544 bytes), 7 new matches, 18 improvements

smart_moves.text: Modeling grCn_803E1D38 as a struct with named subarrays enabled typed access to arwing_gobj, arwing_type, arwing_group, anim_ids, positions, and dialog_joints instead of leaving all accesses as anonymous offsets.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grCn_Data definition and replacements such as data->arwing_type[gp->gv.arwing.xC8]

smart_moves.text: Small local helper inlines captured repeated stage/arwing logic and made later code more searchable by role, even though match evidence for each helper is not individually shown in the slice.
Using Vec3 field access for gp->gv.corneria.xE4 improved type expression where the diff shows prior xE4/xE8/xEC scalar-style usage.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grCorneria_801DD674 and grCorneria_801DDCF0 xE4 component changes

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering a large stage file, introduce a local data struct once repeated offsets resolve into coherent tables; typed arrays such as arwing_gobj, arwing_type, arwing_group, positions, anim_ids, and dialog_joints make subsequent functions more reviewable and searchable.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grCorneria_801DDDA8 changes data->indices to data->arwing_type
review_comments: reviewer still flagged remaining pointer math in grCorneria_801E25C4

decomp_lessons.lesson: Reviewer feedback favors project macros for common expressions: manual absolute-value branches should be checked against ABS, and similar MIN/MAX/CLAMP opportunities should be considered when the expression matches.
evidence_refs: review_comment discussion_r3034048457: reviewer suggests `f32 angle = ABS(gp->gv.arwing.xE8);` and notes MIN, MAX, CLAMP

decomp_lessons.lesson: Raw pointer math against a void/u8 ground-var blob is review-sensitive; if the layout can be recovered, use a typed temporary/local struct or real fields instead.
evidence_refs: review_comment discussion_r3034041253: `Is it possible to fix this pointer math?` on grCorneria_801E25C4

decomp_lessons.lesson: Raw __assert calls in recovered code should be checked for HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG-style macro forms when the file, line, and assertion string are visible.
evidence_refs: review_comment discussion_r3034038681: reviewer says `This should probably use HSD_ASSERT`

naming_conventions.convention: Corneria-local helper names use the grCn_ prefix for static/local helpers and data symbols, while exported or address-based functions retain grCorneria_80xxxxxx names.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: helper definitions and grCorneria function bodies

naming_conventions.convention: Recovered data fields mix semantic names where roles are evident from usage with address/offset-style names where semantics remain unclear.
Short locals follow common role names such as gp, gobj, jobj, idx, pos, scale, and arwing where role is clear.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used narrowly in several newly recovered functions to influence stack shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR evidence shows usage and bot progress, but does not include narrow objdiff rationale for each PAD_STACK instance; treat as a tactic requiring verifier evidence under current standards.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Some repeated numeric stage-width expressions were factored into local f32 temporaries such as half, likely to shape codegen and improve readability.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grCorneria_801E03C8 and grCorneria_801E0678 use `f32 half = ...` before HSD_JObjSetTranslateX
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No explicit reviewer or objdiff explanation is loaded for these temporaries.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Switch statements were used for id/type/state dispatch in stage segment and arwing behavior functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Evidence supports use in this PR but does not prove these are globally canonical forms beyond existing standards.

review_feedback.target: grCorneria_801E08CC raw `__assert("grcorneria.c", 0x9AC, "0")` in hunk also containing grCorneria_801E01A8/grCorneria_801E03C8/grCorneria_801E0678

review_feedback.comment: Is it possible to fix this pointer math?
review_feedback.target: grCorneria_801E25C4 using `u8* v = gv` and offset writes/reads such as v+4, v+0xC, v+0x18

review_feedback.comment: not 100% required, but for cases like this there's `f32 angle = ABS(gp->gv.arwing.xE8);` same for MIN, MAX and CLAMP
review_feedback.target: grCorneria_801DE568 manual absolute-value branch for angle

review_feedback.comment: not really a comment, just wondering that most of these are f32s, how come they're being assigned as s32s 🤔
review_feedback.target: grCorneria_801DE8E4 and nearby code assigning through s32 casts into fields that appear to include f32/Vec3 data

PR 2381 grCorneria_801DE8E4 s32 assignments f32 fields offset_y xE4

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, author, changed files, counts
changed_files.jsonl: all 7 changed file paths and line counts
diff_excerpt/raw_diff: large but truncated diff excerpt for grcorneria.c
review_comments.md: 4 loaded review comments with hunks

notes: PR body is empty/null, so author intent is inferred only from title, diff, bot report, and comments.
raw_diff and raw_pr_json are truncated; exact changes in headers and small one-line files are not visible beyond changed-file metadata.
Bot report provides match/improvement claims, but no full local build, objdiff, or final post-review diff is loaded.
Review comments show reviewer suggestions/questions, not necessarily whether follow-up fixes were applied before merge.
No unsupported final acceptance status is inferred for the reviewer-requested cleanups beyond the PR metadata showing merged.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use typed local structs to replace repeated stage data offsets
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2381 shows grcorneria progress by introducing grCn_Data/grCn_StageData and using named arrays/fields for Corneria stage data; reviewer feedback on remaining pointer math reinforces that typed fields are preferred when layout can be recovered.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Map raw assert expansions back to project assert macros
accepted_candidate_records.text: A raw `__assert("grcorneria.c", 0x9AC, "0")` drew reviewer feedback that it should probably use HSD_ASSERT, matching the broader standard to recover assert/report macros when file, line, and message are known.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer common expression macros for simple abs/min/max/clamp patterns
accepted_candidate_records.text: Reviewer suggested replacing a manual absolute-value branch with `ABS(gp->gv.arwing.xE8)` and mentioned MIN, MAX, and CLAMP for similar cases.

source_update_candidates.title: grcorneria.c owns Corneria stage/arwing behavior and local grCn data recovery
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2381 evidence indicates src/melee/gr/grcorneria.c contains Corneria stage logic, arwing behavior/spawning, stage segment movement, camera-position checks, dialog-joint handling, and local grCn data modeling such as grCn_StageData and grCn_Data.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: grcorneria match/improvement list
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoping fact for future grcorneria work, but should be curator-reviewed because the loaded diff is truncated and current source may have evolved.

source_update_candidates.title: Pointer math in temporary ground-var blobs should trigger type recovery
source_update_candidates.text: When a function takes a void* or u8* ground-var blob and uses repeated offset writes/reads, reviewers may ask whether a local struct or real field layout can replace the pointer math. This is consistent with typed-fields-over-pointer-math.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grCorneria_801E25C4 offset writes into v
source_update_candidates.reason: This may already be covered by typed-fields-over-pointer-math; curator can decide whether a PR-specific example should be added rather than a new standard.

rejection_notes: Do not treat every PAD_STACK use in this PR as accepted style; the slice lacks per-instance objdiff justification.
Do not infer that reviewer suggestions were all resolved before merge; final post-review diff is not fully loaded.
Do not promote exact header or itgreatfoxlaser.h changes beyond changed-file facts because their diffs are not shown in the loaded excerpt.
Do not infer semantic meaning for x3D8, x444, x458, or other offset-style fields beyond their observed table usage.
